title: Skip Count on a Number Line
essential_question: How can you use a number line to skip count and find how many in all?
strategies: Draw equal jumps on a number line
Each jump = the size of each group
Number of jumps = number of groups

pretest.question: Skip count by 3s on a number line: 0, 3, 6, 9, ?. What comes next?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Louise skip counts by 4 on a number line to find 5 × 4. How many jumps does she draw?
pretest.explanation: 5 × 4 means 5 groups of 4 → 5 jumps of size 4.

learn.title: Skip Counting on a Number Line
learn.content: A number line starts at 0. Each jump is the same size. To find 3 × 4, make 3 jumps of 4: 0 → 4 → 8 → 12. You land on 12, so 3 × 4 = 12. The number you land on is always the product.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Jumps = Groups, Jump Size = Group Size
learn.content: For 5 × 4: make 5 jumps, each jump is size 4. Land on 20. For 4 × 5: make 4 jumps, each jump is size 5. Also land on 20! The jumps connect multiplication to the number line.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 4 Steps to Multiply on a Number Line
learn.content: Step 1: Start at 0. Step 2: Decide jump size (= items per group). Step 3: Jump that many times (= number of groups). Step 4: Read where you land — that is the product.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Reading the Number Line Backward
learn.content: If you know where you landed AND the jump size, you can find the number of jumps. Landed on 24, jumped by 6: 24 ÷ 6 = 4 jumps. This connects multiplication to division (coming later!).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Skip Counting and Word Problems
learn.content: When a word problem says 'each row has 5 chairs' and there are 4 rows, you can skip count by 5 four times: 5, 10, 15, 20. The number line makes the repeated addition visible as equal jumps.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 0은 점프가 아니라 출발점입니다
learn.content: 수직선에서 0은 출발점일 뿐, 점프 횟수에 포함되지 않습니다. 5 × 3을 그릴 때: 0에서 시작 → 첫 번째 점프 → 5 (점프 1) → 10 (점프 2) → 15 (점프 3). 총 3번 점프하여 15에 도착. 흔한 실수: 0을 1번째 점프로 세어 4번 점프하면 20에 도착하는 오류. 팁: 점프(arc, 곡선)를 세고, 도착점(점)을 세지 마세요.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 점프 크기 먼저, 점프 횟수 다음
learn.content: 곱셈식 a × b를 수직선으로 그릴 때 — 첫 번째 인자 a = 점프 횟수, 두 번째 인자 b = 점프 크기. 예: 4 × 5 → 5씩 4번 점프 (5, 10, 15, 20). 흔한 실수: 4씩 5번 점프하면 (4, 8, 12, 16, 20) 답은 같지만 모델이 다르므로 '몇 그룹?' 물으면 틀립니다. 팁: 'b'를 화살표 위에 적고, 'a'번만큼 화살표를 그리세요.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 수직선 곱셈 — 4단계 요약
learn.content: 1단계: 0에서 시작. 2단계: 점프 크기 정하기 (= 한 그룹의 크기). 3단계: 그 크기만큼 점프 — '몇 그룹'만큼 반복. 4단계: 도착한 수 = 곱(product). 역방향: 도착한 수 ÷ 점프 크기 = 점프 횟수 (나눗셈 미리보기). 검증: '점프 호'의 개수 = 첫 번째 인자인지 확인!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

feedback.correct: Yes — six jumps of 2 lands on 12.

try.question: Skip count by 4: 0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20. This shows ? × 4 = 20.
try.explanation: Count the jumps: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20 — that's 5 jumps. So 5 × 4 = 20.
hints: Count how many numbers come after 0.

feedback.incorrect: Count the jumps from 0 to 20 by 4s — there are 5.

try.question: Which multiplication matches: skip count by 9, make 4 jumps?
try.explanation: 4 jumps of 9 = 4 × 9 = 36. Also written as 9 × 4 = 36. Same product.

feedback.correct: Right — both B and C equal 36.

try.feedback_correct: Right — both B and C equal 36.

practice_r2.question: Which number line pattern shows 6 × 3?
choices: A. 3 jumps of size 6, landing on 18
B. 6 jumps of size 3, landing on 18
practice_r2.explanation: 6 × 3 = 18. A shows 3 × 6 = 18 (also correct since 6 × 3 = 3 × 6). B shows 6 × 3 directly. Both land on 18.

feedback.incorrect: Both 3 jumps of 6 and 6 jumps of 3 give 18.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r2.question: A skip-count pattern ends at 45. The jump size is 9. How many jumps? What multiplication does it show?
practice_r2.explanation: 45 ÷ 9 = 5 jumps. 5 × 9 = 9 × 5 = 45. Both B and C are correct.

feedback.correct: Right — 5 jumps, and both B and C are correct.

practice_r2.feedback_correct: Right — 5 jumps, and both B and C are correct.

hints: Line up the digits — ones, tens, hundreds.
Carry when a column total reaches 10.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 600 − 247.
practice_r2.explanation: 600−247: borrow across zeros. 0−7 → 10−7=3 (borrow); tens 0−4 → 10−5=5; hundreds 5−2=3. Result: 353.
hints: Borrowing across zeros is tricky — work right to left.
Once you borrow from the hundreds, the tens become 10 and then 9 after lending to ones.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.3 Lesson 3.3 'Skip Count on a Number Line' pp.109-112
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Multiplication Using a Number Line
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: A number line shows: 0, 8, 16, 24, 32. What number was used for skip counting? How many jumps?
practice_r3.explanation: Each step increases by 8. Count the stops: 8, 16, 24, 32 — four jumps.
hints: Find the difference between consecutive stops: 8 − 0 = 8.
Then count how many stops there are after 0.

practice_r3.question: Toby reads 2 books per month. In a picture graph, each 📖 = 2 books. Month 1 shows 3 book pictures. How many books did Toby read in Month 1?
practice_r3.explanation: 3 jumps of 2 on the number line: 2, 4, 6. Product = 6.
hints: Each picture = 2 books. There are 3 pictures.

practice_r3.question: Two students use number lines. Ava makes 9 jumps of size 4. Omar makes 4 jumps of size 9. Who lands farther?
choices: C. They land on the same spot
practice_r3.explanation: Ava: 9 × 4 = 36. Omar: 4 × 9 = 36. Same product — same spot.

feedback.correct: Right — both land on 36. Commutative property!

practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right — both land on 36. Commutative property!
